description: Complete the htmlsight MVP: real Ultralytics training backend, custom multi-task model, real predict, mAP evaluation, Bootstrap docs source, and parallel dataset generation.

userStories.title: Ultralytics training backend
userStories.description: Replace the TrainingUnavailableError stub in model/train.py with a real Ultralytics YOLO training call. The CLI `train` command (without --dry-run) should actually launch a YOLOv8 training run.
acceptanceCriteria: train_model(config) calls ultralytics.YOLO with the correct model size and data.yaml path
TrainingConfig fields (epochs, batch_size, image_size, optimizer, lr, momentum, weight_decay, seed, device, workers, mosaic, flip_lr, patience) are forwarded to ultralytics train()
When ultralytics is not installed, a clear UltralyticsUnavailableError is raised with install instructions
Unit tests mock ultralytics.YOLO and verify the correct parameters are passed
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: Implemented _load_ultralytics() using sys.modules sentinel pattern to support monkeypatching in tests. Maps TrainingConfig fields to ultralytics train() kwargs.

userStories.title: Custom PyTorch Dataset loader for multi-task attributes
userStories.description: Create src/ia_visao_web/model/dataset_loader.py with a PyTorch Dataset that loads YOLO .txt labels and the corresponding JSON sidecar attrs file, yielding both for multi-task training.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WebComponentDataset(root, split) class extending torch.utils.data.Dataset
__len__ returns number of images in the split
__getitem__ returns dict with keys: image (PIL or tensor), boxes (list of YOLO label dicts), attrs (list of attribute dicts from JSON sidecar), image_id (str)
When a sidecar .json is missing, raise a clear error naming the missing file
When torch is not installed, importing the module works but instantiating raises TorchUnavailableError
Tests use a tmp_path fixture with 2 synthetic samples (no real Playwright needed)
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: WebComponentDataset checks torch at instantiation (sentinel pattern from train.py). No class inheritance from torch.Dataset needed — duck-typing sufficient for DataLoader. Module eviction (monkeypatch.delitem) required in test helper for isolation.

userStories.title: Multi-task attribute loss
userStories.description: Implement the real combined loss in model/loss.py: L_total = λ_cls*L_cls + λ_box*L_box + λ_tag*L_tag + λ_disp*L_display + λ_role*L_role + λ_hc*L_haschld. Attribute losses are cross-entropy for tag/display/role and BCE for has_children.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement AttributeLoss(tag_classes, display_classes, role_classes) in model/loss.py
attribute_loss(preds, targets, weights) returns a scalar tensor summing CE losses weighted by LossWeights
Attribute losses are computed only on positive assignments (mask parameter)
When torch is not installed, importing works but instantiating AttributeLoss raises TorchUnavailableError
Tests verify: loss > 0 with random predictions, gradient flows through all heads, loss is 0 when predictions are perfect
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: AttributeLoss(tag_classes, display_classes, role_classes) implements CE for tag/display/role and BCE for has_children. __call__(preds, targets, weights, mask) computes losses only on positive mask entries and returns a weighted scalar. Returns torch.tensor(0.0) when mask has no positives. Tests that require real torch use pytest.importorskip and are skipped when torch is absent — correct behavior for environments without torch.

userStories.title: Real predict command from trained weights
userStories.description: Replace the predict stub (detections: []) with a real implementation that loads a trained YOLO model and returns actual detections. CLI `predict <image.png>` should use the model when weights exist.
acceptanceCriteria: Add predict_image(image_path, weights_path) function to eval/predict.py
Loads ultralytics YOLO from weights_path, runs inference, parses Results to detection_to_json format
CLI predict command accepts optional --weights path; when weights_path is None or file missing, returns detections: [] with a warning
attrs dict in each detection has tag, display, role, has_children keys (stubbed to None until multi-task model is trained)
Tests monkeypatch ultralytics.YOLO to return fake Results and verify JSON shape
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: predict_image(image_path, weights_path) added to eval/predict.py using the same _load_ultralytics() sentinel pattern. CLI predict command accepts optional --weights; when missing returns detections:[] with warning; UltralyticsUnavailableError also handled gracefully. attrs dict stubbed with tag/display/role/has_children=None. Boxes parsed via boxes.xyxy/conf/cls.tolist() for easy mocking.

userStories.title: CLI eval command with mAP metrics
userStories.description: Implement the `eval` CLI command that computes mAP@50 and mAP@50-95 per class on a dataset split using ultralytics val(), plus attribute accuracy from JSON sidecars.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI: `ia-visao-web eval --dataset <path> --weights <path> --split test` runs evaluation
Uses ultralytics model.val() to compute mAP@50 and mAP@50-95 per class
Computes attribute accuracy (tag, display, role, has_children) from sidecar files vs predictions
Outputs a JSON report: {mAP50, mAP50_95, per_class: [...], attr_accuracy: {...}}
When ultralytics or weights are missing, prints clear error and exits with code 1
Tests monkeypatch ultralytics and verify report shape and content
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: evaluate_model(dataset_path, weights_path, split) in eval/evaluator.py calls ultralytics model.val() for mAP metrics and predict_image per sidecar for attr accuracy. CLI eval command accepts --dataset, --weights, --split, --output; exits code 1 on missing weights or ultralytics. UltralyticsUnavailableError imported from predict.py to share the same class with cli.py. _compute_attr_accuracy matches predicted vs GT attrs by position up to min(len(gt), len(pred)). ruff B904: raise ... from exc required inside except clause.

userStories.title: Bootstrap docs fetcher
userStories.description: Add `dataset fetch-docs` CLI command that downloads Bootstrap 5.3 component documentation pages and stores them as HTML in data/sources/bootstrap-docs/ for use as real HTML sources in dataset generation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/ia_visao_web/sources/fetch_bootstrap_docs.py
Fetches at minimum: buttons, cards, forms, modals, navbars, alerts, tables, accordions, tabs pages from Bootstrap 5.3 docs
Saves each as {component}.html in the output directory
Skips already-downloaded files (--force to re-download)
CLI: `ia-visao-web dataset fetch-docs --output data/sources/bootstrap-docs`
Tests mock HTTP calls (urllib or requests) and verify files are written
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: fetch_docs(output, force) in sources/fetch_bootstrap_docs.py uses urllib.request.urlopen (stdlib, no extra deps). BOOTSTRAP_DOC_PAGES dict maps 9 component names to Bootstrap 5.3 doc URLs. Skips existing files by default; --force re-downloads. Tests patch urlopen at module level. CLI dataset fetch-docs added to dataset_app sub-typer.

userStories.title: Parallel dataset generation
userStories.description: Add --workers N option to `dataset build` to use concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor for parallel Playwright rendering, enabling faster generation of 3000+ image datasets.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --workers option (default 1) to `dataset build` CLI command
When --workers > 1, use ProcessPoolExecutor to render pages in parallel
Each worker gets its own Playwright browser context to avoid sharing across processes
Progress is reported to stdout (e.g., Rendered 120/3000 images)
--synthetic-only still works with --workers (parallelizes PIL image generation)
Tests verify that worker=1 and workers=2 produce the same deterministic output for same seeds
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: Added --workers N option (default 1) to dataset build. workers=1 uses sequential loop; workers>1 uses ProcessPoolExecutor from concurrent.futures imported at module level. Module-level _PROCESS_RENDERER global ensures each worker process creates its own renderer on first use. _build_sample_worker is a module-level function (required for pickling). Progress reported as 'Rendered X/N images' for both modes. Tests monkeypatch ProcessPoolExecutor with a SyncExecutor that calls fn() directly to avoid real subprocess spawning.

userStories.title: Integration test and final docs update
userStories.description: Add a full end-to-end integration test that covers: generate 5 synthetic images, write dataset, validate it, create training plan, and run predict stub. Update README and CLAUDE.md with current state.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/test_full_pipeline.py covers: dataset build (synthetic-only, 5 images) -> dataset validate -> train --dry-run -> predict on one image
Test passes without Playwright, torch, or ultralytics installed
README.md updated: correct test count, current feature checklist, known limitations
CLAUDE.md updated with any new commands, patterns, or gotchas discovered during this ralph run
pytest passes, ruff passes, mypy passes
userStories.notes: tests/integration/test_full_pipeline.py covers: build (synthetic-only, 5 images) -> validate (min-train-instances=0) -> train --dry-run -> predict (no weights, empty detections). Also fixed _fixture_detections() navbar width from 600 to 300 to fit smallest viewport (375px). README.md updated with 76 tests, full feature checklist, eval command, --workers example. CLAUDE.md updated with new commands, architectural decisions for ProcessPoolExecutor pattern, and updated verification results.
